title: Repoint opus shorthand to Claude Opus 4.8 with pinned 4.7 alias
description: Update SHORTHAND_MAP in the model resolver so `opus` resolves to claude-opus-4-8, and add an `opus-4-7` shorthand that resolves to claude-opus-4-7 so existing pins keep working. Update model-resolver.test.ts accordingly. This sprint needs no SDK change and works on the installed @anthropic-ai/sdk 0.39.0 because the model id is a free string the API accepts server-side.

successCriteria.description: resolveProviderModel('opus') returns { provider: 'anthropic', modelId: 'claude-opus-4-8' }, asserted by an updated model-resolver unit test.
resolveProviderModel('opus-4-7') returns { provider: 'anthropic', modelId: 'claude-opus-4-7' }, asserted by a new model-resolver unit test for the pinned alias.
The sonnet and haiku shorthands and the full-id passthrough for claude-opus-4-7 keep resolving to their current values, asserted by the existing resolver tests staying green.
npm run build completes with exit code zero after the resolver change, producing an updated dist for the npm-linked cockpit.
npm run test introduces no new failures beyond the documented flaky 37-tool-count baseline, with the model-resolver suite passing.

nonGoals: Do not modify the sonnet or haiku shorthand mappings.
Do not upgrade @anthropic-ai/sdk or modify the Anthropic adapter in this sprint.
Do not change role-to-model assignments in defaults.ts; roles already use the `opus` shorthand and inherit 4.8 automatically.
Do not remove the existing full-id passthrough behavior for claude-opus-4-7.
stopConditions: Stop when the opus->4.8 and opus-4-7->4.7 resolver tests pass and the full suite shows only the flaky baseline failures.
Stop when only src/orchestrator/model-resolver.ts and src/orchestrator/model-resolver.test.ts have changed.
definitionOfDone: The `opus` shorthand in SHORTHAND_MAP resolves to claude-opus-4-8 and a new `opus-4-7` shorthand resolves to claude-opus-4-7; model-resolver.test.ts asserts both mappings and the existing sonnet/haiku and full-id passthrough assertions stay green; no SDK or adapter files change; the package builds and the full unit-test suite shows no new failures beyond the documented flaky baseline.
assumptions: claude-opus-4-8 is a dateless pinned snapshot, so no dated suffix is required.
Roles in defaults.ts reference the `opus` shorthand (not a hardcoded id), so repointing the shorthand updates them transitively.
generatorNotes: Edit src/orchestrator/model-resolver.ts SHORTHAND_MAP (line ~22-26): change `opus: { provider: 'anthropic', modelId: 'claude-opus-4-7' }` to modelId 'claude-opus-4-8'; ADD `'opus-4-7': { provider: 'anthropic', modelId: 'claude-opus-4-7' }`. You MAY also add `'opus-4-8': { provider:'anthropic', modelId:'claude-opus-4-8' }` for symmetry (optional). In src/orchestrator/model-resolver.test.ts: the test 'resolves opus to anthropic/claude-opus-4-7' (line ~6-9) and the resolveModel('opus') assertion (line ~127) must change to claude-opus-4-8; ADD a test asserting resolveProviderModel('opus-4-7') -> claude-opus-4-7. KEEP the existing passthrough tests at line ~117 and ~139 (resolveProviderModel('claude-opus-4-7') / resolveModel('claude-opus-4-7')) — full-id passthrough still yields claude-opus-4-7 via the default-anthropic branch, so those remain valid. Do NOT touch the SDK or adapter. Conventional commit: bober(sprint-1): repoint opus shorthand to claude-opus-4-8 + pin opus-4-7.
evaluatorNotes: Read src/orchestrator/model-resolver.ts and confirm opus->claude-opus-4-8 and a new opus-4-7->claude-opus-4-7 entry. Run npx vitest run src/orchestrator/model-resolver.test.ts and confirm the updated opus assertion plus the new opus-4-7 test pass, and the sonnet/haiku/passthrough tests still pass. Run npm run typecheck and npm run build (exit 0). Run npm run test; only the 2 flaky 37-tool-count timeout baseline failures are tolerated. Confirm git diff touches only model-resolver.ts and its test. No SDK/adapter changes expected.
